spec_match.rationale: The catalog now matches the current CSS Conditional Level 5 draft by using unquoted identifier syntax and listing both registered names: anchor-position-follows-transforms and single-axis-scroll-container.

interactive.rationale: Search, status filters, the detection meter, and the custom probe all run against real CSS.supports('named-feature(<ident>)') calls.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The concept still explains the registry boundary promised by the parent feature page and now frames names as CSS identifiers rather than strings.

edge_cases.rationale: The custom probe distinguishes unrecognized identifiers, which return false, from quoted or otherwise invalid input, which is reported as an invalid CSS identifier.

references.rationale: The page links to ChromeStatus and the CSS Conditional Level 5 named-feature() section, and each registry row links back to the spec section instead of listing unsupported proposed entries without sources.

design_system.rationale: The touched helper copy, meter, and registry paragraphs now use local CSS classes with shared design tokens rather than inline styles.

summary: Repaired after the page was found to teach CSS.supports('named-feature("name")') and to claim only one registry entry. The catalog now uses identifier syntax, includes the current two-entry registry, and closes the stale proposed-feature-link question by removing unsourced proposed rows.
